summary: Matched sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.c by replacing the assembly object with a C implementation of GObj_InitUserData and GObj_RemoveUserData, adding prototypes/header dependencies, and propagating the new symbol names across many existing assembly callsites. The change also refined HSD_GObj field names/types so user-data state is represented as an unsigned byte and stored in user_data rather than a generic data field.
what_changed: Removed the old assembly implementation asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.s from the normal text object list and replaced it with src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.o in obj_files.mk.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.c with matching C implementations for GObj_InitUserData and GObj_RemoveUserData.
Added gobjuserdata.o to EPILOGUE_UNSCHEDULED in e_files.mk.
Updated gobjuserdata.h to include global.h and gobj.h, define HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE as (u8)-1, and declare GObj_InitUserData and GObj_RemoveUserData.
Updated HSD_GObj in gobj.h: user_data_kind changed from s8 to u8, and the generic data field was renamed to user_data.
Renamed many assembly branch-link comments/calls from func_80390B68 to GObj_InitUserData across fighter, item, ground, menu, text, and baselib assembly files.
Updated include/functions.h placeholder comments from func_80390B68/func_80390BE4 to GObj_InitUserData/GObj_RemoveUserData.
smart_moves: Kept callsite evidence synchronized by renaming all known assembly references to GObj_InitUserData after matching the function, making future cross-references searchable by semantic name instead of address-style func_80390B68.
Changed user_data_kind to u8 and defined HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE as (u8)-1, aligning the sentinel with unsigned byte storage and comparisons visible in the implementation.
Renamed HSD_GObj.data to user_data, which makes the relationship between GObj_InitUserData/GObj_RemoveUserData and the struct field explicit.
Preserved assert behavior in the C match using extern string labels lbl_80408590, lbl_804085A0, and lbl_804085D0 plus the observed source line numbers 40 and 99.
Registered the newly matched source object in obj_files.mk while retaining epilogue tracking in e_files.mk, covering both build replacement and epilogue bookkeeping.
decomp_lessons: When a small baselib utility is matched, immediately propagate the recovered function name through assembly callsites; even unchanged assembly becomes much more useful for future decomp work.
Sentinel constants for byte-sized struct fields may need explicit casts, such as (u8)-1, to match signedness and generated comparisons.
Struct field renames are part of decomp progress: replacing a generic field name like data with user_data can clarify ownership and remove ambiguity for later matches.
For assert-bearing library functions, matching may require keeping string labels as externs when the string data is not yet fully named or moved.
A matched C file must be wired into the same object position previously occupied by its assembly counterpart to preserve link order and matching.
naming_conventions: Recovered baselib gobj user-data functions use the GObj_ prefix: GObj_InitUserData and GObj_RemoveUserData.
The no-user-data sentinel is named HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE and is typed as (u8)-1.
HSD_GObj user-data storage is named user_data, paired with user_data_kind and user_data_remove_func.
The PR leaves unrelated unknown functions and labels as func_*/lbl_* where no stronger evidence is present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.o with src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjuserdata.o in TEXT_O_FILES to test the C implementation in the original link slot.
Used exact assert calls in GObj_InitUserData and GObj_RemoveUserData, including source line constants 40 and 99, to match control flow and literals.
Implemented GObj_RemoveUserData with an early return when user_data_kind equals HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE, then asserts if user_data_remove_func is NULL before invoking it.
After the remove callback, GObj_RemoveUserData resets user_data_kind to HSD_GOBJ_USER_DATA_NONE and clears user_data to NULL; the diff does not show clearing user_data_remove_func.
Updated assembly call annotations without changing opcodes: many `bl func_80390B68` lines became `bl GObj_InitUserData`, confirming the symbol name rather than changing behavior.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type the GObj user_data_remove_func callback signature.
Search for remaining references to func_80390BE4 or GObj_RemoveUserData callsites after PR 214.
Check later baselib gobj matches that depend on HSD_GObj.user_data and user_data_kind being u8.
Look for PRs that replace extern assert string labels lbl_80408590/lbl_804085A0/lbl_804085D0 with named strings or rodata.
